title: Aina Berg

infobox.value: |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Representing  [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") | | | | [Olympic Games](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_Summer_Olympics "Swimming at the Summer Olympics") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [1920 Antwerp](/wiki/1920_Summer_Olympics "1920 Summer Olympics") | [4×100 m freestyle relay](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_4_%C3%97_100_metre_freestyle_relay "Swimming at the 1920 Summer Olympics – Women's 4 × 100 metre freestyle relay") | | Bronze medal – third place | [1924 Paris](/wiki/1924_Summer_Olympics "1924 Summer Olympics") | [4×100 m freestyle relay](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1924_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_4_%C3%97_100_metre_freestyle_relay "Swimming at the 1924 Summer Olympics – Women's 4 × 100 metre freestyle relay") | |

This biographical article related to a Swedish swimmer is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Aina_Berg&action=edit).

This article about a Swedish Olympic medalist is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Aina_Berg&action=edit).

(7 January 1902 – 7 October 1992) was a Swedish [freestyle](/wiki/Freestyle_swimming "Freestyle swimming") swimmer who won bronze medals in the 4 × 100 m freestyle relay at the [1920](/wiki/1920_Summer_Olympics "1920 Summer Olympics") and [1924 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1924_Summer_Olympics "1924 Summer Olympics").[[1]](#cite_note-1) In 1920 she also competed in the individual 100 m and 300 m events, but failed to reach the finals. Berg was the national 100 m champion in 1921–1926 and her national record stood from 1921 until 1932.[[2]](#cite_note-sr-2)[[3]](#cite_note-sok-3)

Evans, Hilary; Gjerde, Arild; Heijmans, Jeroen; [Mallon, Bill](/wiki/Bill_Mallon "Bill Mallon"); et al. ["Aina Berg"](https://web.archive.org/web/20161202221131/http://www.sports-reference.com/olympics/athletes/be/aina-berg-1.html). *Olympics at Sports-Reference.com*. [Sports Reference LLC](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference"). Archived from [the original](https://www.sports-reference.com/olympics/athletes/be/aina-berg-1.html) on 2 December 2016.

["Aina Berg"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160825234609/http://sok.se/idrottare/idrottare/a/aina-berg.html). *SOK.se* (in Swedish). [Swedish Olympic Committee](/wiki/Swedish_Olympic_Committee "Swedish Olympic Committee"). Archived from [the original](http://sok.se/idrottare/idrottare/a/aina-berg.html) on 25 August 2016.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Swedish swimmer (1902–1992)

Aina Berg

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Personal information | |
| Born | 7 January 1902  Gothenburg, Sweden |
| Died | 7 October 1992 (aged 90)  Gothenburg, Sweden |
| Sport | |
| Sport | Swimming |
| Club | SK Najaden, Göteborg |
| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Representing  [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") | | | | [Olympic Games](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_Summer_Olympics "Swimming at the Summer Olympics") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [1920 Antwerp](/wiki/1920_Summer_Olympics "1920 Summer Olympics") | [4×100 m freestyle relay](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_4_%C3%97_100_metre_freestyle_relay "Swimming at the 1920 Summer Olympics – Women's 4 × 100 metre freestyle relay") | | Bronze medal – third place | [1924 Paris](/wiki/1924_Summer_Olympics "1924 Summer Olympics") | [4×100 m freestyle relay](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1924_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_4_%C3%97_100_metre_freestyle_relay "Swimming at the 1924 Summer Olympics – Women's 4 × 100 metre freestyle relay") | | |

**Aina Berg** (7 January 1902 – 7 October 1992) was a Swedish [freestyle](/wiki/Freestyle_swimming "Freestyle swimming") swimmer who won bronze medals in the 4 × 100 m freestyle relay at the [1920](/wiki/1920_Summer_Olympics "1920 Summer Olympics") and [1924 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1924_Summer_Olympics "1924 Summer Olympics").[[1]](#cite_note-1) In 1920 she also competed in the individual 100 m and 300 m events, but failed to reach the finals. Berg was the national 100 m champion in 1921–1926 and her national record stood from 1921 until 1932.[[2]](#cite_note-sr-2)[[3]](#cite_note-sok-3)
